observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to trace the differences of the Cash Local Treasury and Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts. We further recommended that Management henceforth conduct properly the monthly reconciliation between cash records as against accounting records in compliance with Section 63 (F) of the LTOM.

We recommended that Management immediately implement the programs/ projects/activities in which the said bank accounts were set-up in order to fully utilize the available resources and to avoid these accounts of becoming dormant.  We further recommended that Management revisit the purpose of opening these current accounts and take appropriate actions in accordance with the above-stated provisions.

We recommended that Management require Disbursing Officer to settle the remaining cash accountabilities of ₱73,409.58.  We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer institute internal controls in the handling, custody and management of cash maintained by the Disbursing Officer.  Lastly, we recommended Management strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that Management investigate the significant delays in the remittance of collections by its collectors and install adequate internal control system in the handling of cash and collections. We further recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor all collectors/tellers towards the turnover of collections in compliance with Section 60 of the LTOM and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

Lastly, we recommended that the Management initiate steps in assuring that collections by the Municipal Treasurer are deposited intact and daily in compliance with Section 62(C) of the LTOM and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 by ensuring that the Municipal Treasurer be constantly provided with the means to do so such as, but not limited to, provision of safe and proper motor vehicle.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the monthly BRS and immediately submit to the Audit Team for verification pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011.  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, prepare the bank reconciliation statement separately for each of the existing depository accounts of the Municipality in order to facilitate reconciliation between the book and bank balances of all accounts as at a given period.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to religiously and diligently comply with the provisions of Section 63 of the LTOM.  We further recommend that the Management make a periodic monitoring on the compliance with the above-stated provisions.

We recommended that Management assess the manpower needs of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and initiate steps towards compliance with DBM LBC No. 137 pertaining to position titles in local government units.  We further recommended that Management immediately stop the practice of designating Job-Order personnel as collecting officers of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the AOs concerned be directed for the immediate renewal of their respective fidelity bonds.  We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor the fidelity bond of the AOs  and make sure its renewal prior to the date of expiration in accordance with the provisions of P.D. 1445 to continue to function in their capacity as accountable officers and avoid administrative and criminal liabilities.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GSO-Designate prepare separate property cards on all classes of PPE in the form as prescribed in the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate ledger cards of all PPE accounts.  Lastly, we recommended that Management issue an executive order establishing the Municipality’s Inventory Committee and henceforth conduct annual physical inventory for all PPEs and subsequently prepare all physical inventory reports.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant immediately prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and lapsing schedule for the Depreciation Expenses accounts pursuant to Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445 and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and make sure that such accounts are reconciled with the amounts in the PPE lapsing schedule.

We recommended that the Management require the General Services Officer to conduct an inventory of the local roads under their jurisdiction and henceforth, perform reconciliation of the local road inventory and road map with those recorded in the “Road Networks” account.  We further recommended that Management strictly follow the accounting and reporting guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor identify the specific parcels of land recorded at a lumped amount of ₱64,083,255.00 in order to give credibility and present fairly the amounts reported in the Municipalities books of accounts. We recommended that, thereafter, Management prioritize the registration, transfer and titling of these parcels of land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Management check the agreement if there are conditions that need to be complied with in its utilization and thereafter return the unexpended balance in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended the Management submit an explanation/justification for the non-implementation of priority projects for CY 2021.  We also recommended that Management always ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit an explanation as to the non-compliance with the aforecited provisions including submission of documentary requirements, if any, to support the corresponding disbursements.  We further recommended that Management stop the practice of implementing generic or lump-sum appropriations on identified development projects unless further approved and authorized by the Sanggunian.  Lastly, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged against the 20% Development Fund which shall include, among others, project location, specific project/program description and specific implementation schedule.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit the lacking documentary requirements supporting the final payment on the acquisition of the lot.  We further recommended that Management provide an explanation as to why the final payment was released to the landowner even if the title to the property was not yet transferred in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the MDRRMO, formulate and implement its comprehensive LDRRMP in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and conduct judicious planning and scheduling to ensure implementation of its disaster prevention PPAs.  We further recommended that Management release and use the LDRRMF only for those identified PPAs in the LDRRMP.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent issuances particularly on the propriety of charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF.  We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to diligently check the purpose of disbursements to ensure correctness in the charging of expenses in relation to funding source and its specific appropriations.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the noted lapses.  We further recommended that henceforth, Management submit the required financial transaction documents on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for prompt verification purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Management desist from adopting this practice of procuring goods and services through direct contracting and paying such by way of reimbursement to the concerned Municipal Officials and employees and instead effectively implement and conform its procurement activities as laid out in the Annual Procurement Plan. We further recommended Management strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check which is directly paid to the suppliers, contractors, dealers and creditors.

Lastly, it is further recommended that Management withhold the prescribed taxes in all of its procurement of goods or services in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately make the necessary adjustments in its books and provide the Audit Team the adjusting journal entries.  We further recommended that henceforth, Management record its financial transactions using the Revised Chart of Accounts as embodied in COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We recommended that the Management procure the common-use supplies and equipment from the Cebu Regional Depot of PS-DBM in accordance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011, AO No. 17 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.   We also recommended that Management comply with the documentary requirements stated in GPPB Resolution No. 17-2020 for those CSE items which are not available in the PS-DBM at the time of procurement before pursuing the procurement from other sources.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC submit the documentary requirements of these contracted suppliers in order to regularize these procurement transactions.  Henceforth, we recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions stated in the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 concerning the guidelines on the procurement of goods & services through Alternative Mode of Procurement particularly under Negotiated Procurement-SVP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the auditors the documents and reports due to be submitted for CY 2019. We also recommended, henceforth, that Management submit the required financial transaction documents on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for prompt verification purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended management the immediate submission of Subsidiary Ledgers of all Business Income accounts including the Contracts to Sell and/or Lease Contract Agreements for its Municipal Cemetery Operations as of December 31, 2019 for further audit evaluation.  We, likewise, recommended that Lease Contract Agreements between the Municipality and market stallholders be renewed taking into consideration the rental rates authorized under the SB Ordinance and ensure the due execution of the stipulations provided therein.

We recommended that management strictly enforce compliance of supply and property management as embodied in Chapter 7 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Volume I especially on the recognition of supplies and materials for stock as inventories.

We recommended that the Accounting Office continue with the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balance to come up with the details of the overdue amount and ensure that these are fully remitted to the BIR. We further recommended that henceforth a monthly reconciliation of remittances against amounts withheld be conducted to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.

We recommended management to account the under/over remittance, take appropriate actions and inform the audit team of its subsequent developments.  Moving forward, we recommended the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure timely and accurately the remittances of GSIS, PhilHealth & HDMF premiums, contributions and loan amortizations to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.

We recommended that management immediately implement the projects / programs / activities funded by the National Government and other agencies in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those PPAs; otherwise, return these funds to the Source Agencies.  Also, the Municipal Accountant should be directed to initiate timely liquidation and return the unexpended balances to the source agency after the completion of the intended projects/programs/activities in compliance with the aforecited regulation.

We recommended management that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council re-appropriate those savings/unexpended balances for eligible development projects under the 20% Development Fund pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 99-66 to attain maximum utilization of the fund.
We further recommended management to closely monitor the implementation of all 20% development projects to ensure its timely completion. We further advised that the Municipal Planning & Development Coordinator effectively carry out its functions to avoid incurrence of further delays in the completion of projects.

We recommended that management stop the practice of charging expenditure items that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures and strictly comply with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in order to maximize the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that management stop the practice of allocating and/or charging janitorial services to the LDRRMF and henceforth strictly observe proper programming and utilization of such fund in compliance with Republic Act No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipality assess its existing organizational structure so that a reasonable number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job-order personnel.  We further recommended that all hired job-order personnel be covered by a Contract of Service/Job-Order.

We recommended that management henceforth strictly follow the documentary requirements upon the release of salaries & wages of job-order personnel as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 particularly on requiring proper authorization and identification of those individuals receiving other than the payees.

We recommended that management henceforth initiate steps to insure all insurable properties of the Municipality in the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS through compliance with the provisions of Republic Act No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-001 starting calendar year 2019.

We recommended management to revisit their Revenue Code and conduct general revision of property assessment every three (3) years to conform with Section 219 of R.A. 7160.  We further recommended that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the 2016 RIRR of R.A. 9184 on the conduct of pre-procurement conference & pre-bid conference and COA Circular No. 2012-001 particularly on requiring documentary requirements and attaching these to its respective disbursement vouchers before effecting payments.

We recommended that management submit henceforth copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

We recommended that management submit to the COA Audit Team the required GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) for calendar year 2019 in the form as prescribed by PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and henceforth submit such document within the deadline set by COA Circular No. 2014-01.

We recommended that the management establish a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system through issuance an executive order creating the M&E Team and henceforth comply with the provisions of Item 5.0 of PCW-DBM-DILB-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01, as amended.

We recommended that management immediately create the LYDC and the LYDO pursuant to the provisions of Republic Act No. 10742 and submit to the audit team proofs of such for our auditorial review and evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that all collections should be remitted and deposited intact with the Government Depository Bank.

We also recommend to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-382 to prevent the accumulation of big balances of the Cash on Hand thereby exposing the funds to misappropriation and theft if not deposited.

We recommend that management submit authority to collect and set aside the enumerated collections otherwise the amount of P465,383.83 be transferred to the General Fund and form part unappropriated surplus of the municipality.

We also recommend that expenditures should be in accordance with the fundamental principles of disbursements as enumerated in Section 4 of P.D. 1445.
We recommend that a copy of the journal entry voucher in adjusting and correcting the erroneous entry be provided to the Auditor.

We recommend that the projects outlined from funds of  various national government agencies be fully implemented so that the intended  beneficiaries be able to utilize the project and its purpose and objective be attained.

We recommend that all projects to be  funded  out  of  the  Fund  should
contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management  outcomes  and  shall  partake  the  nature  of
investment or capital expenditures.

We  also  recommend  that  the  chief  executive  ensure   the   optimal
utilization of the 20% Development  Fund  to  be  able  to  achieve  the
desirable outcomes for which the fund was created and to  implement  the
sanctions provided in DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum  provisions  of  Joint
Circular 1-11 for the programming of development projects.

We recommend that the hiring of the job  order  workers  be  limited  to
emergency or intermittent work and should not, in any case, be made to
perform functions which are part of the job description of the agency’s
existing regular employees.

We also recommend that CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No.1,  2.  2017  be
strictly  followed  specially  the  transitory  provisions  otherwise   the
sanction provided therein shall be meted.

We recommend that authority  for  the  municipality  to  open  a  Time
Deposit Account be submitted  otherwise  the  amount  of  P1,743,987.47  be
returned to the source were it was taken.

We also recommend that the accumulated interest income existing  in  the
Trust Fund be transferred to the  General  Fund  and  be  available  for
budget.

We further recommend that funds received  in  the  Trust  Fund  should
always be made available for the purpose it was received.

We recommend that the Municipality set aside 1%  of  the  IRA  in the
Annual Budget for the strengthening of the LCPC. Also  plans  and  policies
should  be  formulated  on  child  survival,  development  protection   and
participation. Meetings should regularly  be  conducted  duly  supported
with the Minutes of the Meeting.

We further recommend  for  the  strict  compliance  to  the  mandate  of
Memoranda Circular Nos. 2012-120 and 2008-126 for the full functionality of
the LCPC.

We recommend that the municipality should  comply  with  all  applicable
rules and regulations of Republic Act No.9003 or  the Ecological Solid  Waste
Management  Act  of  2000  to  ensure  protection  of  public   health   and
environment by  closing  the  open  dump  sites  and  operating  a  sanitary
landfill.

We also recommend that the municipality should  prioritize  expenses  in
the Solid Waste Management plan to ensure the efficient management of solid
waste  generated  within  its  jurisdiction  thus  putting  an  end  to  the
continued existence of open dump sites.
Request from DSWD, the source agency authorization to disburse the balance to legitimate beneficiaries. The balance or any undistributed ESA Fund should be returned to DSWD.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: Give priority in looking for and appointing a permanent and qualified Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer who will run and manage the LDRRMO, a mandatory office, and thus give focus on the implementation of the disaster programs of the Municipality in compliance with Rule 1, Section 3 (e) and Rule 5, Section 4 (1) of the IRR of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: The position was posted and published several times by the HRMO but still no applicant for the position. A designate is being done to head the LDDRMO.

observations.recommendation: Continuous monitoring of the collections of the collectors be made and that a system should be remitted daily to the liquidating officer and Section 29 of the NGAS-Local Manual be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: Collectors were advised to remit their collections and ordered to remit their collections daily as mandated in Section 29 of the NGAS-Local Manual.

observations.recommendation: Discrepancies found in the monthly balances in the books of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be corrected and reconciled including the 2012 year end balance for reliable financial statements presentation.
observations.managementAction: General Fund Balances were already reconciled. However, the other Fund Balance differences were not traced due to lack of documents in the accounting office for the years prior to 2009.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer to submit a certified list of taxpayers, to the accountant, showing prior years and current year’s unpaid real property taxes so that these could be correctly recorded in the books of the ensuing year. Further, we recommend that the Accountant reconcile the balances of the Real Property Tax Receivable against the Special Education Tax Receivable to ascertain the validity of the account balances.
observations.managementAction: Computerization of the Treasurer/Accounting/Assessor’s office was partially implemented in order to generate a timely and accurate reports on real property taxes.

observations.recommendation: Secure the Certificate of Title/Torrens Title in the name of the Municipality to assure ownership of the lot for a cemetery site.
observations.managementAction: The matter has already been processed by the Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: Advise PO’s/NGO’s with accumulated bank deposits to withdraw the same and remit said amount to the Municipal Treasurer as partial satisfaction of the loan. All outstanding overdue loans should be restructured and the term of payment should be made monthly for easy monitoring.
observations.managementAction: A task force was created to monitor all PO’s/NGO’s with outstanding obligation.

observations.recommendation: Projects outlined from funds of various national government agencies be fully implemented so that the intended beneficiaries will be able to enjoy the benefits derived from the projects and it’s purposes and objectives can be attained.
observations.managementAction: Prioritizing projects were done in order to implement those projects that is more useful to the constituent due to many projects that was unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: Require the immediate liquidation of the said cash advances through demand letters to be signed by the Municipal Mayor or his representative. In case of refusal by the employees to liquidate or settle their accountabilities, direct the withholding of salaries to be applied in satisfaction of the unsettled cash advance pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Lastly, clearances for retiring, resigning or transferring officials be deferred until such time that they have liquidated their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were sent to the accountable officers/employees.

observations.recommendation: Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their records as mandated by the regulation to fully ascertain the amount of the PPE. It is also recommended that the accountant should maintain property and PPE ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation between inventory and accounting records.
observations.managementAction: There is an on going reconciliation between the two records (Accounting and General Services Office).

observations.recommendation: Exert extra effort to collect the Loan Receivable amounting to P2,296,928.58 and the outstanding cash advances (Due to Officers and Employees) totaling P4,790,286.87. Furthermore, impose the sanction in Section 5 of the Memorandum of Agreement, for those borrowers who failed to pay despite repeated demands made. Municipal Accountant to trace the source of the non-moving accounts totaling P2,714,277.11 and to make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued but sanctions were not imposed.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the  projects  outlined  from  funds  of  various
        national government agencies  be  fully  implemented  so  that  the
        intended beneficiaries will be able to enjoy the  benefits  derived
        from  the  projects  and  it’s  purposes and objectives can be attained.

We recommend that Management be required to  request  from  DSWD,  the
        source  agency  authorization  to  disburse  the   balance   to   legitimate
        beneficiaries. The balance or any undistributed ESA Fund should  be
        returned to DSWD.

We recommend that the municipality ensure that  the   20%  development
funds  be  optimally  utilized  to  help  attain  desirable   socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes and see  to  it  that  the
programs and projects funded out of the funds are  in  accordance  with  the
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum and the provisions of Joint Circular 1-11 for  the
programming of development project.

      We also recommend that programs/projects/activities budgeted  in  lump
sum should have the prior individual approval by the Sanggunian pursuant  to
the Supreme Court ruling in the case of Hon. Gabriel Luis Quisumbing et  al.
vs. Hon. Gwendolyn F. Garcia,  et  al,  GR  No.  175527  promulgated  on  08
December 2008.

We  recommend  that  Management  give  priority  in  looking  for  and
appointing a permanent and qualified Disaster Risk Reduction and  Management
Officer who will run and manage the LDRRMO, a  mandatory  office,  and thus
give  focus  on  the  implementation  of  the  disaster  programs   of   the
Municipality in compliance with Rule 1, Section 3 (e) and Rule 5, Section  4
(1) of the IRR of RA 10121.

We recommend that management minimize the incurrence of excessive and unnecessary catering services/meals/snacks to a reasonable amount. If the same cannot be avoided, we recommend that management ensure that the meals, snacks, catering services for special occasions, conferences and seminars are duly supported with guest lists or prospective attendees in order to arrive at the most proximate number of attendees. Likewise, cost of meals and snacks to be spent for entertaining visitors/guests of their corresponding offices be charged to the monthly representation allowance of department heads.
observations.managementAction: Expenses on foodstuffs had been minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should adhere strictly to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 93 of PD 1445 requiring payments for government transactions be made by check and payable directly to the creditor or a cash advance shall be granted to the disbursing officer for a specific purpose. Stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials who earlier paid the transaction in cash. Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash through the cash advance issued to them.
observations.managementAction: Expenses were already paid directly to the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management cause the submission of LDRRMFIP prepared in accordance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the grant of cash advance should be limited to the requirements for three (3) months and additional amount may be granted only after liquidation of the previous cash advance and upon the certification of the Agency Accountant stamped or printed on the disbursement voucher (DV). We also recommend that cash advance vouchers should have covering transmittal letters enumerating the documents that shall be submitted by the Agency Accountant or authorized representative in a sealed envelope, to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader within seven (7) days after release of check for post audit.
observations.managementAction: The submission of the Cash Advance documents to the auditor within seven (7) days from release was followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that expenses intended for the operation, repair and sports development activities of the school be charged in the Special Education Fund in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 of Republic Act 5447 and Section 273 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Expenses pertaining to the SEF were already charged to the same fund.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that a written authority from the Office of the Solicitor General with the concurrence of the Commission on Audit granting conformity in the hiring of a private lawyer be submitted otherwise the expenses charged to this account could be declared as without authority and will be disallowed in audit. It is also recommended to stop the practice of hiring private lawyers until such time that a written authority could be secured.
observations.managementAction: The practice of hiring lawyers was already stopped.
